_metadata.description: Physical model configuration for multirotor drone sizing tool.

atmosphere._description: Standard ISA atmospheric conditions at the operating altitude. Density rho is computed from altitude using the ISA model if altitude_m > 0, otherwise the provided rho_kg_m3 value is used directly.

propeller_model._description: Hybrid analytical + corrective coefficient model based on actuator disk theory. CT and CP are non-dimensional thrust and power coefficients calibrated for the target propeller family. Diameter and pitch are supplied from mission inputs (manufacturer datasheet). The pitch_to_diameter_ratio values below are kept for reference only and are no longer used in computation.
propeller_model._CT_note: Thrust coefficient. Typical range for multirotor propellers: 0.009 to 0.015.
propeller_model._CP_note: Power coefficient. Typical range for multirotor propellers: 0.004 to 0.007.
propeller_model._slip_factor_note: Propeller slip (ratio of induced velocity loss). Typical range: 0.10 to 0.20.
propeller_model._pitch_hover_note: [Reference only — not used in computation] Pitch-to-diameter ratio for hover efficiency. Typical: 0.5 to 0.65.
propeller_model._pitch_speed_note: [Reference only — not used in computation] Pitch-to-diameter ratio for forward flight. Typical: 0.7 to 1.0.

battery._description: LiPo battery physical model. The nominal cell voltage is 3.7V per cell. Effective voltage is computed as: V_eff = cell_count_S x 3.7. The depth of discharge (DoD) defines the usable fraction of the total stored energy. Mass fraction bounds constrain the battery sizing optimization.
battery._energy_density_note: Specific energy of the battery pack in Wh/kg. Typical LiPo range: 150 to 250 Wh/kg.
battery._cell_voltage_note: Nominal voltage per LiPo cell. Used to compute pack voltage from cell count S.
battery._dod_note: Usable fraction of total battery energy. 0.8 means 80% of stored energy is available. Remaining 20% is kept to preserve battery health.
battery._mass_fraction_min_note: Minimum battery mass as a fraction of (m_struct + m_payload). Below this, the battery is undersized.
battery._mass_fraction_max_note: Maximum battery mass as a fraction of (m_struct + m_payload). Above this, the added weight penalty exceeds the energy benefit.

drivetrain._description: Drivetrain efficiency model. If eta_global is non-null, it takes priority over the product of individual efficiencies (eta_motor x eta_esc x eta_propeller). Individual values are kept for reference and documentation purposes.
drivetrain._eta_motor_note: Brushless motor efficiency. Typical range: 0.85 to 0.92.
drivetrain._eta_esc_note: Electronic Speed Controller efficiency. Typical range: 0.93 to 0.97.
drivetrain._eta_propeller_note: Propeller aerodynamic efficiency. Typical range: 0.65 to 0.80.
drivetrain._eta_global_note: Override for global drivetrain efficiency. If set to a float (e.g. 0.62), it replaces the product of individual efficiencies. Set to null to use computed value.

coaxial_correction._description: Thrust loss correction for coaxial rotor configurations (x8 only). In a coaxial pair, the lower rotor operates in the disturbed wake of the upper rotor, resulting in a thrust loss. The effective thrust per motor pair is: T_pair = 2 x T_single x thrust_loss_factor. Not applied for quad, hexa, or octo configurations.
coaxial_correction._thrust_loss_note: Coaxial thrust efficiency factor. Typical range: 0.80 to 0.90. Applied only when enabled is true and configuration is coaxial (x8).

constraints._description: Design constraints used to validate the sizing results and trigger warnings in the output report.
constraints._TWR_min_note: Minimum acceptable thrust-to-weight ratio. Below this value, the drone cannot safely maneuver or handle wind disturbances.
constraints._TWR_max_note: Maximum acceptable TWR. Above this, the drone is oversized and likely too heavy or inefficient.
constraints._battery_mass_note: Hard upper limit on battery mass in kg. Driven by structural and payload constraints.
constraints._propeller_diameter_note: Maximum propeller diameter in inches. Driven by airframe geometry and ground clearance.
